content.text: I found the likely root cause in the NIO socket write path: a partial `SocketChannel.write` can discard unwritten bytes from the intermediate buffer, corrupting chunk framing or truncating fixed-length responses. I’ll add a focused temporary regression test around partial writes next.

content.text: I found the likely root cause in the low-level NIO socket writer: plain `NioSocket.write(BufferData)` consumes bytes from Helidon buffers into an 8 KB `ByteBuffer`, but calls `SocketChannel.write` only once per fill, so any partial channel write silently drops the remaining bytes. That exactly explains malformed chunked framing and content-length hangs. I’m adding a focused regression test to reproduce that partial-write behavior before changing the fix.

content.text:
Implemented the fix in:

- `common/socket/src/main/java/io/helidon/common/socket/NioSocket.java`

Root cause:
- `NioSocket.write(BufferData)` copied bytes into an internal `ByteBuffer`, flipped it, then called `SocketChannel.write(writeBuffer)` only once.
- `SocketChannel.write` is allowed to perform a partial write even in blocking mode.
- When that happened, the remaining bytes in the temporary `ByteBuffer` were discarded while the source `BufferData` had already been consumed, corrupting HTTP output. This explains malformed chunked bodies, missing CRs, and fixed-length responses hanging due to missing bytes.

Fix:
- After flipping the internal write buffer, loop until `writeBuffer.hasRemaining()` is false before continuing.

I also wrote a focused regression test to reproduce partial `SocketChannel.write` behavior, but per instruction removed it before finishing.

Validation:
- Attempted to run the new test with Maven.
- The repository requires a newer Java toolchain than available in the container:
  - installed: Java 21
  - project compilation target: release 26
- Maven failed before tests could run because `release version 26 not supported`.
- The temporary test was deleted afterward.

Only the production fix remains modified.
